station_card.title: Chest discomfort after lunch in a 58-year-old man
station_card.heading: CANDIDATE INFORMATION AND TASKS
candidate_information: You are working in a general practice clinic.
Your next patient is David Nguyen, aged 58 years.
He has come in because of chest discomfort that started after lunch.
tasks: Take a concise, relevant and focused history.
Ask the examiner for any examination findings or observations you require.
Explain your likely concerns to the patient.

reading_time_plan.label: Reading focus
items: Identify the setting, recipient and four tasks.
Plan a focused chest pain history before you enter.
Decide which observations or examination findings you may request.
Leave time to explain your concern and management plan.
Do not assume the timing after lunch explains the symptoms.

opening_line.speaker: David
opening_line.text: Doctor, I think it is just indigestion, but my chest feels really tight.

reveal_actions.label: Pain story
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.response: It started 45 minutes ago while I was carrying boxes. It is in the centre of my chest, heavy and tight, 8 out of 10. It goes into my left arm and jaw and it is not settling.

reveal_actions.label: Associated symptoms
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.response: I feel sweaty, sick and short of breath. I am scared.

reveal_actions.label: Risk factors
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.response: I smoke. I have high blood pressure and high cholesterol. My father had a heart attack.

reveal_actions.label: Ideas and concerns
reveal_actions.speaker: David
reveal_actions.response: I thought it was indigestion because it started after lunch. I am worried about missing work.

reveal_actions.label: Observations and examination
reveal_actions.speaker: Examiner
reveal_actions.response: David looks pale and sweaty. Heart rate 104, blood pressure 155/92, respiratory rate 22, oxygen saturation 95% on room air, temperature 36.8 °C and pain 8/10. His chest is clear. There is no new murmur and no calf swelling. Troponin is not available quickly enough for safe clinic management.

response_action.label: Response to your plan
response_action.speaker: David
response_action.response: An ambulance? Can I just drive myself? Is it really that serious?

finish_action.confirmation_text: You will not be able to reveal more information in this attempt. Review will unlock after you finish.
